summary: Researched two AI agent memory papers (ActMem arxiv:2603.00026 and M2A arxiv:2602.07624). Found both papers, extracted architecture details, benchmark results, and practical insights for our LanceDB-based memory system. Corrected two inaccuracies in the task brief: ActMem does NOT use episodic/semantic layers (it uses a single causal+semantic KG), and the 40% improvement claim was not found in either paper (largest actual gain is ~23% relative in M2A).
findings: ActMem (2603.00026): Core insight is the 'retrieval-reasoning gap' — NaiveRAG gets 84.86% retrieval accuracy but only 61.54% QA. ActMem uses a unified causal+semantic knowledge graph with counterfactual two-pass retrieval, NOT an episodic/semantic layer split.
ActMem results: +14.78% retrieval and +12.55% QA vs LightMem (DeepSeek-V3 on ActMemEval); +6.96% on LongMemEval. Causal edges use PMI filtering (tau=0.8) to suppress spurious causality.

M2A (2602.07624): True dual-layer — Layer 1 RawMessageStore (append-only log, all modalities) + Layer 2 SemanticMemoryStore (compressed entries with evidence_ids pointers back to Layer 1). Two-agent design: ChatAgent + MemoryManager (isolated to prevent context pollution).

M2A retrieval: tri-path (dense + BM25 + cross-modal) fused via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60). Progressive narrowing (semantic → raw via pointers) is the highest-value single component: ablation shows -16 pts without it.
M2A results: Qwen3-VL-8B +23.5% relative vs best baseline; GPT-4o-mini +8.4 abs pts vs A-MEM. The 40% improvement claim was NOT found in either paper.
LanceDB insight #1: Add evidence_ids pointer fields to semantic memory entries linking back to raw observations — currently missing from our system.
LanceDB insight #2: Fuse FTS5 + dense vector via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60) instead of independent fallback — handles exact-match queries (names, dates) that pure semantic search misses.
LanceDB insight #3: Progressive narrowing (semantic retrieval first, drill into raw records via pointers) is the highest-ROI retrieval pattern per M2A ablation study.
